message:
feat(core,abi): port XSI fmtmsg classification + formatting to core::fmtmsg (bd-fmt-epic)

Lifts the pure logic of unistd_abi.rs::fmtmsg (~50 lines: classification
bit decode, severity-name lookup, message formatting) into a tiny
pure-safe core::fmtmsg module.

New core surface:
  pub const MM_PRINT: i64 = 0x100;
  pub const MM_CONSOLE: i64 = 0x200;
  pub fn should_print(classification: i64) -> bool
  pub fn severity_name(severity: i32) -> &'static str
  pub fn format_fmtmsg_message(label, severity, text, action, tag) -> Vec<u8>

The formatter unconditionally emits the `: SEVERITY: ` and
`\nTO FIX: ... \n` separators (matching the XSI / Solaris reference
output shape) — empty fields render as empty between them.

abi shim: ~52 lines -> ~16. The five 5x-repeated CStr::to_str dance
collapses into a single closure; the match-severity ladder moves to
core::fmtmsg::severity_name; the format!() macro becomes one
format_fmtmsg_message call.

Verified:
  - 11 new core::fmtmsg unit tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests still pass

Closes bd-fmt-epic.
